- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Analysis notebook — growing record of ReAct steps with provenance.
2	
3	Each step records what tool was used, what it found, and which evidence
4	IDs produced the result. build_synthesis_context() outputs markdown with
5	[CITE:ev_xxx] tokens that point to ORIGINAL sources.
6	"""
7	
8	import logging
9	import os
10	import time
11	import uuid
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13	
14	from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import AnalysisEntry
15	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolResult
16	
17	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
18	
19	# LAW VI: every knob env-tunable. Per-step character budget for the result digest that
20	# ``summary_for_llm(include_results=True)`` hands the planner. 0 disables the digest.
21	PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS_DEFAULT = 600
22	
23	
24	def _summary_result_chars() -> int:
25	    raw = os.getenv(
26	        "PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS",
27	        str(PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS_DEFAULT),
28	    )
29	    try:
30	        return max(0, int(raw))
31	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
32	        logger.warning(
33	            "[analysis_notebook] PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS=%r is not an int; "
34	            "using default %d",
35	            raw, PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS_DEFAULT,
36	        )
37	        return PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS_DEFAULT
38	
39	
40	def _scalar(value) -> str:
41	    """Render a statistic VERBATIM — never reformat a number.
42	
43	    ``str(1234567.89)`` -> ``'1234567.89'``. (``f'{v:g}'`` would emit ``1.23457e+06``
44	    and silently mangle the very value the planner needs; strict numeric verification
45	    downstream compares digits.)
46	    """
47	    return str(value)
48	
49	
50	def _result_digest_lines(result: ToolResult, budget: int) -> list[str]:
51	    """Planner-facing digest of ONE successful step's payload, capped at ``budget`` chars.
52	
53	    Priority order = information density: statistics (the numbers) -> insights -> markdown
54	    fills the remainder. Nothing is dropped by KIND (weight-and-consolidate, not filter);
55	    when the budget runs out the omission is DISCLOSED inline ("+N more") rather than
56	    silently swallowed, because a silently-dropped computed number is exactly the failure
57	    this digest exists to end.
58	    """
59	    out: list[str] = []
60	    spent = 0
61	
62	    def _emit(line: str) -> bool:
63	        """Emit if it fits. Returns False once the budget is exhausted."""
64	        nonlocal spent
65	        if spent + len(line) > budget:
66	            return False
67	        out.append(line)
68	        spent += len(line)
69	        return True
70	
71	    stats = result.statistics if isinstance(result.statistics, dict) else {}
72	    if stats:
73	        pairs = [f"{k}={_scalar(v)}" for k, v in stats.items()]
74	        shown: list[str] = []
75	        for i, pair in enumerate(pairs):
76	            candidate = ", ".join([*shown, pair])
77	            if len(f"stats: {candidate}") > budget and shown:
78	                out.append(
79	                    f"stats: {', '.join(shown)} (+{len(pairs) - i} more omitted for length)"
80	                )
81	                spent += budget  # budget is spent; markdown/insights yield to the numbers
82	                break
83	            shown.append(pair)
84	        else:
85	            if shown:
86	                _emit(f"stats: {', '.join(shown)}")
87	
88	    for insight in (result.insights or []):
89	        if not _emit(f"insight: {insight}"):
90	            break
91	
92	    md = (result.markdown or "").strip()
93	    if md:
94	        remaining = budget - spent
95	        if remaining > 0:
96	            flat = " ".join(md.split())
97	            if len(flat) > remaining:
98	                flat = flat[:remaining] + "…"
99	            out.append(f"result: {flat}")
100	
101	    return out
102	
103	
104	@dataclass
105	class AnalysisStep:
106	    """One step in the ReAct loop."""
107	
108	    step_number: int
109	    reasoning: str
110	    tool_name: str
111	    result: ToolResult
112	    elapsed_seconds: float
113	
114	
115	class AnalysisNotebook:
116	    """Accumulates analysis steps and produces synthesis context with provenance."""
117	
118	    def __init__(self, query: str, evidence_ids: list[str]):
119	        self._query = query
120	        self._evidence_ids = evidence_ids
121	        self._steps: list[AnalysisStep] = []
122	        self._data_points: list[dict] = []
123	        self._started_at = time.monotonic()
124	
125	    def add_step(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:
126	        """Add a completed step and accumulate its data points."""
127	        self._steps.append(step)
128	        if step.result.success and step.result.data_points_produced:
129	            self._data_points.extend(step.result.data_points_produced)
130	
131	    @property
132	    def steps(self) -> list[AnalysisStep]:
133	        return list(self._steps)
134	
135	    @property
136	    def data_points(self) -> list[dict]:
137	        return list(self._data_points)
138	
139	    @property
140	    def has_data(self) -> bool:
141	        return len(self._data_points) > 0
142	
143	    @property
144	    def step_count(self) -> int:
145	        return len(self._steps)
146	
147	    @property
148	    def successful_steps(self) -> int:
149	        return sum(1 for s in self._steps if s.result.success)
150	
151	    def get_all_source_evidence_ids(self) -> list[str]:
152	        """Collect all unique evidence IDs across all successful steps."""
153	        ids = []
154	        seen = set()
155	        for step in self._steps:
156	            if step.result.success:
157	                for eid in step.result.source_evidence_ids:
158	                    if eid not in seen:
159	                        ids.append(eid)
160	                        seen.add(eid)
161	        return ids
162	
163	    def build_synthesis_context(self) -> str:
164	        """Build markdown context with [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens for synthesis.
165	
166	        Priority rules:
167	        1. If interpret_results exists, lead with it (the analytical prose)
168	        2. Follow with extract_numeric_data (structured data for reference)
169	        3. Skip comparison_table and statistical_summary entirely —
170	           the interpretation already incorporates their findings, and
171	           raw pivot tables are unreadable noise for the section writer
172	
173	        This ensures the section writer sees ANALYSIS first, then
174	        supporting data — never raw stat dumps or sparse pivot tables.
175	        """
176	        if not self._steps:
177	            return ""
178	
179	        has_interpretation = any(
180	            s.tool_name == "interpret_results" and s.result.success
181	            for s in self._steps
182	        )
183	
184	        # Tools to SKIP when interpretation exists (it supersedes them)
185	        # PQ-4: Keep comparison_table — it provides supplementary
186	        # context the writer can use for structured comparisons
187	        skip_when_interpreted = {
188	            "statistical_summary", "query_evidence_sql",
189	        }
190	
191	        sections = []
192	
193	        # Pass 1: interpretation first (if it exists)
194	        if has_interpretation:
195	            for step in self._steps:
196	                if step.tool_name == "interpret_results" and step.result.success:
197	                    sections.append("### Research Analysis")
198	                    sections.append("")
199	                    sections.append(step.result.markdown)
200	                    sections.append("")
201	                    break
202	
203	        # Pass 2: supporting raw data (extraction only, skip stats/tables)
204	        for step in self._steps:
205	            if not step.result.success:
206	                continue
207	            if step.tool_name == "interpret_results":
208	                continue  # Already added above
209	
210	            if has_interpretation and step.tool_name in skip_when_interpreted:
211	                continue  # Interpretation supersedes these
212	
213	            tool_title = step.result.tool_name.replace("_", " ").title()
214	            sections.append(f"### {tool_title}")
215	            sections.append("")
216	            sections.append(step.result.markdown)
217	
218	            if step.result.insights:
219	                sections.append("")
220	                for insight in step.result.insights:
221	                    sections.append(f"- {insight}")
222	
223	            sections.append("")
224	
225	        return "\n".join(sections)
226	
227	    def summary_for_llm(self, include_results: bool = False) -> str:
228	        """Compact summary for the ReAct planner's next decision.
229	
230	        W4 (2026-07-11): ``include_results`` is OPT-IN and defaults to False, so the
231	        long-standing consumer (``react_agent.py``:7875) keeps its byte-identical string.
232	        With ``include_results=True`` (the OutlineAgent decide prompt,
233	        ``outline_agent.py``:1163) each SUCCESSFUL step also emits a RESULT DIGEST.
234	
235	        Why: before this, ``summary_for_llm()`` was the ONLY notebook accessor fed to the
236	        outline decide LLM, and it printed only ``{n}. {tool} [{status}] ({elapsed}s) —
237	        {reasoning[:60]}``. A successful ``execute_python`` therefore recorded no gap (so
238	        nothing was disclosed) while its computed value — present in ``.statistics``,
239	        ``.insights`` AND ``.markdown`` — was unreachable by the planner: an UNDISCLOSED
240	        UNREACHABLE RESULT, and a tool that was strictly net-negative (it burned a turn and
241	        an OpenRouterClient for zero observable effect).
242	
243	        WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): nothing is
244	        dropped by kind. Statistics come first (they are the numbers), then insights, then
245	        markdown fills whatever character budget is left. The per-step budget is env-tunable
246	        (LAW VI) via ``PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS`` (default 600); set it to 0 to
247	        suppress the digest entirely.
248	
249	        This digest is PLANNER-FACING ONLY. It does not create a render path: exploratory
250	        ``execute_python`` output remains BARRED from the report, which renders computed
251	        numbers only through the verified ``[#calc:]`` / ``ModelSpec`` lane.
252	        """
253	        lines = [
254	            f"Query: {self._query}",
255	            f"Evidence pool: {len(self._evidence_ids)} pieces",
256	            f"Steps completed: {self.step_count} "
257	            f"({self.successful_steps} successful)",
258	            f"Data points extracted: {len(self._data_points)}",
259	            "",
260	            "Steps so far:",
261	        ]
262	
263	        budget = _summary_result_chars() if include_results else 0
264	
265	        for step in self._steps:
266	            status = "OK" if step.result.success else "FAILED"
267	            lines.append(
268	                f"  {step.step_number}. {step.tool_name} [{status}] "
269	                f"({step.elapsed_seconds:.1f}s) — {step.reasoning[:60]}"
270	            )
271	            if budget > 0 and step.result.success:
272	                lines.extend(
273	                    f"     {ln}" for ln in _result_digest_lines(step.result, budget)
274	                )
275	
276	        return "\n".join(lines)
277	
278	    def to_entries(self) -> list[AnalysisEntry]:
279	        """Convert successful steps to AnalysisEntry contracts."""
280	        entries = []
281	        for step in self._steps:
282	            if not step.result.success:
283	                continue
284	
285	            entries.append(AnalysisEntry(
286	                entry_id=f"analysis_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
287	                analysis_type=step.result.tool_name,
288	                title=step.reasoning[:100],
289	                markdown=step.result.markdown,
290	                source_evidence_ids=step.result.source_evidence_ids,
291	                statistics=step.result.statistics,
292	                image_base64=(
293	                    step.result.charts[0].get("image_base64", "")
294	                    if step.result.charts
295	                    else ""
296	                ),
297	                insights=step.result.insights,
298	            ))
299	
300	        return entries

204	def _canon_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
205	    """Lowercase, punctuation-stripped, stopword-dropped token SET — the canonical form used
206	    ONLY to collapse checklist paraphrases onto one ledger todo (see module note above)."""
207	    import re as _re
208	    words = _re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", str(text or "").lower())
209	    return frozenset(w for w in words if w and w not in _STOPWORDS)

212	def _jaccard(a: frozenset, b: frozenset) -> float:
213	    if not a and not b:
214	        return 1.0
215	    if not a or not b:
216	        return 0.0
217	    inter = len(a & b)
218	    union = len(a | b)
219	    return inter / union if union else 0.0

295	@dataclass
296	class GapTodo:
297	    section: str
298	    aspect: str
299	    needed_kind: str = "coverage"   # "coverage" | "numeric_rows" | "density"
300	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED
301	    attempts: int = 0
302	    source: str = "checklist"        # checklist | tool_failure | agent
303	    disclosure: str = ""
304	
305	    def key(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
306	        return (str(self.section), str(self.aspect))
307	
308	
309	class GapLedger:
310	    """Dedup-by-(section,aspect) EXACT match, falling back to same-section token-overlap
311	    (Jaccard >= threshold) paraphrase collapse; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED + disclosed
312	    on exhaustion.
313	
314	    §-1.3.1 / §-1.1: this ledger is a ROUTING signal (what to search next), never a quality
315	    verdict about the corpus — it names todos, it does not score the pipeline's own output.
316	    """
317	
318	    def __init__(self, max_retries_per_aspect: Optional[int] = None):
319	        self._todos: dict[tuple[str, str], GapTodo] = {}
320	        self._max_retries = (
321	            max_retries_per_aspect
322	            if max_retries_per_aspect is not None
323	            else _gap_retries_per_aspect()
324	        )
325	
326	    def _find_paraphrase(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
327	        """Same-section, token-overlap match against an EXISTING todo (any status). Iter-2
328	        P1-1: collapses re-worded checklist gaps onto the one todo already tracking that
329	        aspect so the per-aspect retry cap actually bites."""
330	        canon_new = _canon_tokens(aspect)
331	        if not canon_new:
332	            return None
333	        threshold = _aspect_dedup_threshold()
334	        best: Optional[GapTodo] = None
335	        best_score = 0.0
336	        for (sec2, asp2), todo in self._todos.items():
337	            if sec2 != section:
338	                continue
339	            score = _jaccard(canon_new, _canon_tokens(asp2))
340	            if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
341	                best, best_score = todo, score
342	        return best
343	
344	    def add(
345	        self, section: str, aspect: str,
346	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
347	    ) -> GapTodo:
348	        section = str(section or "").strip()
349	        aspect = str(aspect or "").strip()
350	        key = (section, aspect)
351	        existing = self._todos.get(key)
352	        if existing is not None:
353	            return existing
354	        paraphrase = self._find_paraphrase(section, aspect)
355	        if paraphrase is not None:
356	            return paraphrase
357	        todo = GapTodo(
358	            section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source,
359	        )
360	        self._todos[key] = todo
361	        return todo
362	
363	    def add_unfillable(
364	        self, section: str, aspect: str, reason: str,
365	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
366	    ) -> GapTodo:
367	        """Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration — 'Summary Table' gap re-fired ~10x over
368	        945s while every fetched row stayed orphaned in the pool): a gap whose named section
369	        can NEVER be routed to retrieval — it does not match any current outline title, and
370	        under a locked required-structure deliverable there is no way to add a new section for
371	        it — is a STRUCTURAL limitation, not a content gap. Recording it UNFILLED immediately
372	        (never PENDING) means ``next_pending()`` never selects it and the decide-loop never
373	        fires ``search_more_evidence`` for it, so the loop cannot burn its turn/wall budget
374	        re-fetching the same unhomeable aspect under a slightly different checklist wording
375	        each round. Still disclosed (§-1.3 — no silent drop), just never retried."""
376	        todo = self.add(section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source)
377	        if todo.status not in ("UNFILLED", "COMPLETE"):
378	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
379	            todo.disclosure = reason
380	        return todo
381	
382	    def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
383	        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))
384	
385	    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
386	        for todo in self._todos.values():
387	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
388	                return todo
389	        return None
390	
391	    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
392	        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
393	        todo.attempts += 1
394	
395	    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
396	        todo.status = "COMPLETE"
397	
398	    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
399	        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
400	        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
401	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
402	            todo.disclosure = reason
403	        else:
404	            todo.status = "PENDING"
405	
406	    @property
407	    def pending_count(self) -> int:
408	        return sum(1 for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "PENDING")
409	
410	    @property
411	    def unfilled(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
412	        return [t for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "UNFILLED"]
413	
414	    @property
415	    def all_todos(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
416	        return list(self._todos.values())
417	
418	    def as_list(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
419	        return [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in self._todos.values()]
420	
421	    def summary_for_llm(self) -> str:
422	        if not self._todos:
423	            return "  (empty — no gaps identified yet)"
424	        lines = []
425	        for t in self._todos.values():
426	            lines.append(
427	                f"  [{t.status}] section={t.section!r} aspect={t.aspect!r} "
428	                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
429	            )
430	        return "\n".join(lines)

445	    budgets: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
446	    disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
447	    # W4: successful execute_python payloads, so a computed value is no longer discarded at
448	    # every exit. EXPLORATORY / NOT RENDERABLE — every row carries renderable=False +
449	    # bar_reason (see _compute_result_check). Telemetry + planner provenance only; this is
450	    # NOT a render path and never reaches a writer prompt.
451	    compute_results: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
452	    turn: int = 0
453	    started_monotonic: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)
454	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None
455	    total_input_tokens: int = 0
456	    total_output_tokens: int = 0
457	    # Iter-2 fix: the deliverable's REQUIRED section titles (empty => no structure lock, the
458	    # common case for the free-form acceptance/real-corpus runs). When non-empty, the outline's
459	    # section STRUCTURE is governed by the caller (S4 ORCH-2 PUSH 1 / `_call_outline`'s own
460	    # `required_sections` conform already enforced this at SEED time) and this module must never
461	    # silently add a 5th section behind the caller's back — see `_tool_update_outline` and the
462	    # auto-assign fallback in `OutlineAgent._execute_tool`.
463	    required_titles: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
464	    # Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus THINNED run: 8 search_more_evidence calls, 31 genuinely NEW
465	    # fetched rows, ZERO landed in any required section — the whole retrieval budget was spent
466	    # re-chasing the SAME unhomeable section label, e.g. "Summary Table", under 8 differently
467	    # worded aspects that each failed the (section,aspect) paraphrase dedup because the ASPECT
468	    # text varied enough while the SECTION stayed constant). Once a section label is proven
469	    # unhomeable (checklist- or agent-initiated), it is banked here (normalized) and vetoed —
470	    # cheaply, before any real fetch — for the rest of the run, regardless of aspect rewording.
471	    unhomeable_sections: set = field(default_factory=set)
472	    _checkpoint_seq: int = 0
473	
474	    def next_ev_offset(self) -> int:
475	        """Highest numeric suffix across current ev_ids, +1. Rows that don't parse as
476	        ``ev_<digits>`` are ignored for the offset computation (never crash the offset)."""
477	        mx = -1
478	        for eid in self.ev_store.keys():
479	            tail = str(eid).rsplit("_", 1)[-1]
480	            if tail.isdigit():
481	                mx = max(mx, int(tail))
482	        return mx + 1
483	
484	    def existing_urls(self) -> set[str]:
485	        urls: set[str] = set()
486	        for row in self.ev_store.values():
487	            if isinstance(row, dict):
488	                u = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip()
489	                if u:
490	                    urls.add(u)
491	        return urls
492	
493	    def disclose(self, text: str) -> None:
494	        self.disclosures.append(text)
495	        logger.info("[outline_agent] %s", text)
496	
497	    def elapsed_seconds(self) -> float:
498	        return time.monotonic() - self.started_monotonic
499	
500	    def checkpoint(self, event: str) -> None:
501	        """Best-effort checkpoint-after-every-mutation (crash-resume). NEVER raises — a
502	        checkpoint failure must not abort the loop (mirrors ``write_cp4_outline_snapshot``'s
503	        own best-effort contract one layer up)."""
504	        if not self.checkpoint_dir:

545	def _plan_field(plan: Any, key: str, default: Any = "") -> Any:
546	    """Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test, not in the original Fable P0/P1 list): plans in
547	    ``workspace.outline_draft`` are NOT a uniform type across the loop's lifetime — the SEED plans
548	    from ``_call_outline`` are ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances, but ``outline_revise.apply_
549	    revision_ops`` (which BOTH ``update_outline`` and the new iter-2 auto-assign call after every
550	    successful search) always returns plain ``dict`` plans (``RevisionApplyResult.new_plans:
551	    list[dict]``). A bare ``getattr(p, 'title', default)`` silently returns ``default`` for a dict
552	    (dicts have no attribute access) — so the FIRST time any update_outline/auto-assign op ran,
553	    every downstream title lookup in THIS module (decide's outline summary, the checklist's
554	    section block, ``inspect_basket``'s assignment check, auto-assign's own section-title
555	    resolver) would have silently gone blank, breaking the very mutation this iteration exists to
556	    fix. Mirrors ``outline_revise._plan_get`` exactly (dict-or-attribute duck typing) — reproduced
557	    here rather than imported to keep this module's OFF-path import graph unchanged (LAW VI/V:
558	    small, side-effect-free helper; not worth a cross-module coupling for 4 lines)."""
559	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
560	        return plan.get(key, default)
561	    return getattr(plan, key, default)
562	
563	
564	def _plan_to_dict(plan: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
565	    if isinstance(plan, dict):
566	        return dict(plan)
567	    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(plan):
568	        return dataclasses.asdict(plan)
569	    return {
570	        "title": _plan_field(plan, "title", ""),
571	        "focus": _plan_field(plan, "focus", ""),
572	        "ev_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "ev_ids", []) or []),
573	        "basket_ids": list(_plan_field(plan, "basket_ids", []) or []),
574	        "archetype": _plan_field(plan, "archetype", ""),
575	        "undersupplied": _plan_field(plan, "undersupplied", False),
576	    }

583	def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
584	    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
585	    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
586	    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
587	    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
588	    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
589	    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
590	    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""
591	
592	    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
593	        import concurrent.futures as _futures
594	        import contextvars as _contextvars
595	
596	        async def _run() -> str:
597	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
598	            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
599	            try:
600	                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
601	                return (resp.content or "").strip()
602	            finally:
603	                if hasattr(client, "close"):
604	                    try:
605	                        await client.close()
606	                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
607	                        pass
608	
609	        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
610	
611	        def _worker() -> str:
612	            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))
613	
614	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
615	            return pool.submit(_worker).result()
616	
617	    return _llm

620	def _offset_renumber(
621	    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], offset: int, existing_ids: set[str],
622	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
623	    """THE sharp seam (design §): renumber ``new_rows`` to ``ev_{offset+i}`` and HARD
624	    fail-loud assert the new id set never intersects ``existing_ids``. A collision here is a
625	    programming bug in the offset computation, not a data condition — it must never be
626	    swallowed (LAW II: fail loudly, never silently degrade)."""
627	    renumbered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
628	    new_ids: set[str] = set()
629	    for i, row in enumerate(new_rows):
630	        new_id = f"ev_{offset + i:03d}"
631	        if new_id in existing_ids or new_id in new_ids:
632	            raise AssertionError(
633	                f"outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED: {new_id!r} already present "
634	                f"(existing_ids has it: {new_id in existing_ids}; batch dup: {new_id in new_ids}). "
635	                "This must never happen — the offset computation has a bug."
636	            )
637	        new_ids.add(new_id)
638	        copied = dict(row)
639	        copied["evidence_id"] = new_id
640	        renumbered.append(copied)
641	    assert new_ids.isdisjoint(existing_ids), (
642	        "outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED post-hoc: new ids intersect existing ids "
643	        f"({sorted(new_ids & existing_ids)})"
644	    )
645	    return renumbered

648	async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
649	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
650	    agent_model: str,
651	    *,
652	    section: str = "",
653	    aspect: str = "",
654	    domain: Optional[str] = None,
655	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
656	    **_ignored: Any,
657	) -> ToolResult:
658	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
659	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
660	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
661	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
662	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
663	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
664	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
665	    if not aspect_text:
666	        return ToolResult(
667	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
668	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
669	            error="missing_aspect",
670	        )
671	
672	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
673	        _is_status_leak,
674	    )
675	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
676	
677	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
678	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
679	    )
680	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
681	    try:
682	        derive_prompt = (
683	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
684	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
685	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
686	            "no quotes.\n\n"
687	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
688	        )
689	        try:
690	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
691	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
692	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
693	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
694	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
695	            resp = await client.generate(
696	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
697	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
698	            )
699	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
700	                resp.content or ""
701	            ).strip() else ""
702	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
703	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
704	            derived = ""
705	    finally:
706	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
707	            try:
708	                await client.close()
709	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
710	                pass
711	
712	    query = derived or aspect_text
713	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
714	        return ToolResult(
715	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
716	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
717	            error="status_leak_aborted",
718	        )
719	
720	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
721	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
722	    )
723	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
724	        merge_retrieval_results,
725	    )
726	
727	    t0 = time.monotonic()
728	    try:
729	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
730	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
731	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
732	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
733	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
734	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
735	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
736	            run_live_retrieval,
737	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
738	            amplified_queries=[query],
739	            domain=domain,
740	            anchor_seed=False,
741	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
742	        )
743	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
744	        return ToolResult(
745	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
746	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
747	            error=str(exc)[:500],
748	        )
749	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
750	
751	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
752	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
753	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
754	
755	    existing_urls = workspace.existing_urls()
756	    deduped_rows = [
757	        r for r in fetched_rows
758	        if str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip() not in existing_urls
759	        or not str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
760	    ]
761	    n_url_dup = len(fetched_rows) - len(deduped_rows)
762	
763	    offset = workspace.next_ev_offset()
764	    existing_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
765	    renumbered = _offset_renumber(deduped_rows, offset, existing_ids)
766	
767	    # S2 stamp pass: chrome delete (content-integrity) + topic-judge fail-open off-topic delete.
768	    kept_rows, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic = await _stamp_and_delete(
769	        renumbered, workspace.research_question, agent_model,
770	    )
771	
772	    for row in kept_rows:
773	        workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row
774	
775	    n_kept = len(kept_rows)
776	    n_deleted = len(deleted_chrome) + len(deleted_offtopic)
777	    disclosure = (
778	        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
779	        f"url-dup dropped {n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
780	        f"(chrome={len(deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
781	    )
782	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
783	
784	    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
785	    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
786	        md_lines.append(
787	            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
788	            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
789	        )
790	    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
791	        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")
792	
793	    return ToolResult(
794	        success=n_kept > 0,
795	        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
796	        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
797	        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
798	        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
799	    )

802	async def _stamp_and_delete(
803	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
804	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
805	    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
806	    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
807	    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
808	    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
809	    if not rows:
810	        return [], [], []
811	
812	    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415
813	
814	    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
815	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
816	    for row in rows:
817	        body = max(
818	            (
819	                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
820	                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
821	                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
822	                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
823	                )
824	            ),
825	            key=len, default="",
826	        )
827	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
828	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
829	        try:
830	            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
831	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
832	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
833	            is_junk, cls = False, ""
834	        if is_junk:
835	            copied = dict(row)
836	            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
837	            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
838	        else:
839	            survivors.append(row)
840	
841	    if not survivors:
842	        return [], deleted_chrome, []
843	
844	    try:
845	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
846	            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
847	        )
848	        if not topic_gate_enabled():
849	            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
850	        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
851	            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
852	        )
853	        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
854	            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
855	        )
856	        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
857	        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
858	        deleted_offtopic = []
859	        for row in dropped:
860	            copied = dict(row)
861	            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
862	            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
863	        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
864	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
865	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
866	        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []

911	
912	async def _tool_update_outline(
913	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace, *, ops: Any = None, **_ignored: Any,
914	) -> ToolResult:
915	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_revise import (  # noqa: PLC0415
916	        apply_revision_ops, parse_revision_ops,
917	    )
918	
919	    if ops is None:
920	        return ToolResult(
921	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
922	            markdown="update_outline requires `ops` (a revision op list).",
923	            error="missing_ops",
924	        )
925	    allowed_ev_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
926	    plan_titles = [str(_plan_field(p, "title", "")) for p in workspace.outline_draft]
927	    raw = ops if isinstance(ops, (dict, str)) else {"ops": ops}
928	    parse_result = parse_revision_ops(
929	        raw, allowed_ev_ids=allowed_ev_ids, plan_titles=plan_titles,
930	    )
931	    if parse_result.parse_failed or not parse_result.ops:
932	        return ToolResult(
933	            success=False, tool_name="update_outline",
934	            markdown=(
935	                f"No ops applied (parse_failed={parse_result.parse_failed}, "
936	                f"rejected={parse_result.rejected})."
937	            ),
938	            error="no_ops_applied",
939	        )
940	
941	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) into apply — mirrors
942	    # `scripts/orchestrator_lab/outline_lab.py`'s `_required` threading. Without this, a
943	    # required_sections-governed outline (e.g. the DRB-72 4-section canonical structure) could
944	    # silently grow a 5th section via an agent-issued `add` op, breaking the caller's exact-N-in-
945	    # order structural contract that `_call_outline` already conformed the seed to.
946	    apply_result = apply_revision_ops(
947	        workspace.outline_draft, parse_result,
948	        required_titles=(workspace.required_titles or None),
949	    )
950	    # Iter-2 fix (found via offline smoke test): ``apply_revision_ops`` ALWAYS returns
951	    # ``list[dict]`` (``outline_revise`` is dict-native; it is not currently wired into
952	    # production composition at all — grep confirms its only callers before this module were
953	    # the offline ``outline_lab.py`` harness). But THIS module's ``workspace.outline_draft`` is
954	    # handed straight back to ``generate_multi_section_report`` as ``parse_result.plans``, and
955	    # every downstream composition site (M-44 primary injection, section-budget code, the
956	    # manifest's ``[p.title for p in multi.outline]``, etc.) does direct ``.title``/``.ev_ids``
957	    # ATTRIBUTE access on real ``SectionPlan`` dataclass instances. Handing dicts downstream
958	    # would ``AttributeError`` the first real composition run the moment any update_outline (or
959	    # the iter-2 auto-assign) op fires. Reconcile back to ``SectionPlan`` HERE — the one seam
960	    # where outline_revise's dict world meets the rest of the pipeline's dataclass world — so
961	    # every OTHER call site in this module (and every downstream consumer) can keep doing plain
962	    # ``SectionPlan`` attribute access unconditionally.
963	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: PLC0415
964	    workspace.outline_draft = [
965	        SectionPlan(
966	            title=str(d.get("title", "")),
967	            focus=str(d.get("focus", "")),
968	            ev_ids=list(d.get("ev_ids", []) or []),
969	            archetype=str(d.get("archetype", "")),
970	            undersupplied=bool(d.get("undersupplied", False)),
971	            basket_ids=list(d.get("basket_ids", []) or []),
972	        )
973	        for d in apply_result.new_plans

1094	        Table' for the actual title 'Summary Table: Application Cases, Impacts, and Risks by
1095	        Industry/Occupation', and the strict-exact match orphaned the fetched rows) a
1096	        normalized substring-containment or high token-Jaccard match — still IDENTITY

1098	        on no match."""

1100	        if not sec_norm:

1103	        for title in titles:

1534	        """Deterministic: empty comparison / zero datapoints / empty SQL / <2 studies -> auto

1537	        never states a quality number)."""
1538	        result = step.result
1539	        if step.tool_name in (
1540	            "statistical_summary", "comparison_table", "meta_analysis", "query_evidence_sql",
1541	        ):
1542	            empty = (
1543	                not result.success
1544	                or (not result.data_points_produced and not result.statistics)
1545	            )
1546	            if empty:
1547	                self.workspace.gap_ledger.add(
1548	                    section="(unassigned)",
1549	                    aspect=f"{step.tool_name} returned no usable rows — need more numeric data",
1550	                    needed_kind="numeric_rows", source="tool_failure",

1552	        # W3 (2026-07-11): a FAILED compute was previously invisible here — the agent moved on with
1553	        # no record that the number it wanted was never derived. Record it UNFILLABLE, not PENDING:
1554	        # a compute failure is NOT retrievable, and a PENDING "(unassigned)" todo is routed to
1555	        # search_more_evidence (decide rule 1, :1182) — which burns real web fetches on an error
1556	        # string and can auto-assign a section literally titled "(unassigned)" (:1681). add_unfillable

1560	            # paraphrase-collapses (Jaccard) against SAME-SECTION todos of ANY status. The

1563	            # to UNFILLED — silently killing a real search. A distinct section label removes the

1565	            self.workspace.gap_ledger.add_unfillable(
1566	                section="(compute)",

1575	    def _compute_result_check(self, step: AnalysisStep) -> None:
1576	        """W4 (2026-07-11): a SUCCESSFUL compute must be DISCLOSED and CARRIED, never silently
1577	        dropped — and must NOT thereby become a render path.
1578	
1579	        Before W4 a successful ``execute_python`` was the worst of both worlds: success meant
1580	        ``_tool_failure_gap_check`` recorded NO gap (so nothing was disclosed), while the value it
1581	        computed was discarded at every exit (``summary_for_llm`` stripped it; the cp4 checkpoint
1582	        keeps only tool_name/reasoning/success; the entry-point return carried no notebook payload).
1583	        An UNDISCLOSED UNREACHABLE RESULT — the tool burned a turn + an OpenRouterClient + a sandbox
1584	        exec for zero observable effect, i.e. it was strictly net-NEGATIVE.
1585	
1586	        What this does: records the computed statistics on the workspace (so they survive the exit,
1587	        see :2049 ``notebook_compute``) and DISCLOSES them as EXPLORATORY.
1588	
1589	        What this deliberately does NOT do (binding invariant, docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md):
1590	        it does not stamp these numbers ``[#calc:]`` and does not hand them to the renderer.
1591	        Exploratory ``execute_python`` output is BARRED from rendering. The verified lane
1592	        (``tradeoff_modeler.ModelSpec`` -> ``quantified_analysis.execute_quantified_model``) accepts
1593	        only ``SourcedInput``s carrying an evidence span (``ev_id`` + ``raw_literal`` +
1594	        ``literal_start``/``literal_end``) and RE-DERIVES the number by re-executing a validated
1595	        formula, pinning ``display_value``; ``verify_modeled_atom`` then re-checks the rendered digits
1596	        against that re-execution. A bare ``{"npv": 1234567.89}`` returned by an LLM-written script
1597	        has no evidence span and no re-derivable formula, so wrapping it in a ModelSpec would FORGE
1598	        provenance through the only hard gate. The number therefore stays planner-facing until the
1599	        verified lane derives it independently; the disclosure below is what keeps that honest
1600	        instead of silent.
1601	        """
1602	        result = step.result
1603	        if step.tool_name not in _CODEGEN_TOOLS or not result.success:
1604	            return
1605	        stats = result.statistics if isinstance(result.statistics, dict) else {}
1606	        if not stats:
1607	            return
1608	
1609	        self.workspace.compute_results.append({
1610	            "turn": step.step_number,
1611	            "tool_name": step.tool_name,
1612	            "reasoning": step.reasoning,
1613	            "statistics": dict(stats),
1614	            "insights": list(result.insights or []),

1616	            "renderable": False,

1619	                "render only via the verified [#calc:] / ModelSpec lane, which re-derives it from "
1620	                "sourced evidence spans"
1621	            ),
1622	        })

1624	        self.workspace.disclose(
1625	            f"execute_python (turn {step.step_number}) computed {len(stats)} value(s) [{pairs}] — "
1626	            "EXPLORATORY ONLY: visible to the planner, BARRED from the report. Rendering a computed "

1805	                        # section behind the caller's exact-N-in-order contract (§9.1.8 /
1806	                        # outline_revise.py `required_titles`).
1807	                        #
1808	                        # Iter-4 P0-1 fix: before giving up, try the SAME mechanical remap
1809	                        # backstop the checklist detector uses — route the fetched rows to the
1810	                        # existing section whose own topic (title+focus) the aspect text
1811	                        # overlaps most. This is what lets an agent-initiated gap (not just a
1812	                        # checklist-named one) still land in a real section instead of
1813	                        # orphaning a genuinely fetched, on-topic row.
1814	                        remap_title = self._best_remap_title(asp)
1815	                        if remap_title is not None:
1816	                            assign_result = await _tool_update_outline(
1817	                                self.workspace,
1818	                                ops={"ops": [{
1819	                                    "op": "reassign", "title": remap_title,
1820	                                    "add_ev_ids": new_ev_ids,
1821	                                }]},
1822	                            )
1823	                            if assign_result.success:
1824	                                resolved_title = remap_title
1825	                                self.workspace.disclose(
1826	                                    f"auto-assign: {sec!r} did not match any locked required "
1827	                                    f"section title, but its vocabulary overlaps existing "
1828	                                    f"section {remap_title!r} the most — remapped and routed "
1829	                                    f"{len(new_ev_ids)} new ev_id(s) there (iter-4 P0-1 fix)"
1830	                                )
1831	                            else:
1832	                                remap_title = None  # fall through to the unhomeable path below
1833	                        if remap_title is None:
1834	                            # Iter-3 P0 fix: this used to fall through to the generic after-fold
1835	                            # recheck+retry cycle below, which re-ran the checklist (which had NO
1836	                            # way to see these orphaned rows as "assigned" since they were never
1837	                            # folded into any section) — so the SAME agent-initiated gap re-armed
1838	                            # itself as PENDING every round it hadn't yet hit the retry cap, and in
1839	                            # parallel the checklist detector kept minting FRESH differently-worded
1840	                            # todos for the same unhomeable target. Both paths compounded into the
1841	                            # measured ~10-round, ~945s degeneration on the real S2S3 corpus. This
1842	                            # gap is now terminated here: UNFILLED immediately, never retried, and
1843	                            # the after-fold recheck below is skipped (nothing changed for it to
1844	                            # re-judge — retrieval structurally cannot help).
1845	                            unhomeable_agent_gap = True
1846	                            reason = (
1847	                                f"{sec!r} does not match any current outline section title, "
1848	                                f"required_titles={self.workspace.required_titles} forbids "
1849	                                "adding a new one, and no existing section's own topic overlaps "
1850	                                f"this facet's vocabulary enough to remap it — {len(new_ev_ids)} "
1851	                                "new ev_id(s) stay in the pool; this is a structural limitation "
1852	                                "(deliverable-level/formatting request), not a retrievable "
1853	                                "content gap — recorded UNFILLED, not retried"
1854	                            )
1855	                            self.workspace.disclose(f"auto-assign SKIPPED: {reason}")
1856	                            existing.status = "UNFILLED"
1857	                            existing.disclosure = reason
1858	                            # Iter-3 P1 fix: bank the section label (see docstring + the
1859	                            # checklist side of this same fix above) so a FUTURE agent-initiated
1860	                            # attempt under different aspect wording is vetoed BEFORE spending a
1861	                            # real fetch.
1862	                            self.workspace.unhomeable_sections.add(_normalize_section_label(sec))
1863	                    # Design control flow: "if search: fold-in + re-check aspect". A successful
1864	                    # FETCH+ASSIGN is not the same claim as "the aspect is now covered" — re-run
1865	                    # the checklist so a fresh judge decides whether this (section, aspect) is
1866	                    # still named deficient against the ENLARGED, NOW-ASSIGNED corpus. Only mark
1867	                    # COMPLETE when the checklist no longer names it; otherwise treat the fetch as
1868	                    # a partial fill and retry/exhaust normally. This avoids a "fetched something,
1869	                    # therefore gap filled" false-complete (the ghost mindset banned by
1870	                    # CLAUDE.md §-1.1). SKIPPED for an unhomeable agent-initiated gap (iter-3 fix
1871	                    # above) — nothing was assigned, so nothing changed for the checklist to
1872	                    # re-judge; re-running it here is what regenerated the runaway.
1873	                    if not unhomeable_agent_gap:
1874	                        still_named = await self._run_checklist(trigger="after_fold")
1875	                        still_deficient = any(
1876	                            t.key() == existing.key() for t in still_named
1877	                        )
1878	                        if still_deficient:
1879	                            self.workspace.gap_ledger.mark_retry_or_unfilled(
1880	                                existing,
1881	                                "search_more_evidence fetched rows but the after-fold checklist "
1882	                                "still names this aspect deficient",
1883	                            )
1884	                        else:
1885	                            self.workspace.gap_ledger.mark_complete(existing)
1886	
1887	        self.workspace.checkpoint(event=f"turn_{self.workspace.turn}:{decision.action}")
1888	        return step
1889	
1890	    # -- main loop ------------------------------------------------------------
1891	
1892	    async def run(self) -> OutlineWorkspace:
1893	        ws = self.workspace
1894	        await self._run_checklist(trigger="seed")
1895	        ws.checkpoint(event="seed_checklist")
1896	
1897	        while ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds:
1898	            ws.turn += 1
1899	            try:
1900	                decision = await self._decide()
1901	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a decide failure ends the loop, not the run
1902	                logger.warning("[outline_agent] decide failed at turn %d: %s", ws.turn, exc)
1903	                ws.disclose(f"decide failed at turn {ws.turn}: {exc}; stopping loop")
1904	                break
1905	
1906	            if decision.action == _FINISH_ACTION:
1907	                deficiencies = await self._run_checklist(trigger=f"finish_attempt_turn_{ws.turn}")
1908	                pending = ws.gap_ledger.pending_count
1909	                budget_remains = ws.turn < self.max_turns and ws.elapsed_seconds() < self.wall_seconds
1910	                if (deficiencies or pending) and budget_remains:
1911	                    ws.disclose(
1912	                        f"finish_outline BOUNCED at turn {ws.turn}: "
1913	                        f"{len(deficiencies)} new + {pending} pending gap(s) remain, "
1914	                        "budget available — continuing loop"
1915	                    )
1916	                    continue
1917	                ws.disclose(
1918	                    f"finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn {ws.turn}: "
1919	                    f"pending={pending}, budget_remains={budget_remains}"
1920	                )
1921	                break
1922	
1923	            await self._execute(decision)
1924	
1925	        # Any todo still PENDING at exit — whether it was retried to the cap or NEVER attempted
1926	        # (turns/wall ran out before the agent ever picked it up) — is honestly UNFILLED, not
1927	        # silently dropped. The two reasons are disclosed distinctly (§-1.3: every gap that
1928	        # wasn't filled is disclosed, never silently absent from the coverage/limitations trail).
1929	        for todo in ws.gap_ledger.all_todos:
1930	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
1931	                reason = (
1932	                    "budget exhausted after retries" if todo.attempts > 0
1933	                    else "budget exhausted before this gap was ever searched"
1934	                )
1935	                todo.status = "UNFILLED"
1936	                todo.disclosure = reason
1937	
1938	        ws.checkpoint(event="exit")
1939	        return ws
1940	
1941	    def _max_retries_display(self) -> int:
1942	        return self.workspace.gap_ledger._max_retries  # noqa: SLF001 — same-module access
1943	
1944	
1945	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1946	# Entry point — wired at the multi_section_generator `_call_outline` seam.
1947	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1948	
1949	async def run_outline_agent_or_legacy(
1950	    research_question: str,
1951	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
1952	    gen_model: str,
1953	    outline_temperature: float,
1954	    outline_max_tokens: int,
1955	    *,
1956	    domain: str = "",
1957	    finding_clusters: Any = None,
1958	    deliverable_spec: Any = None,
1959	    scope_spec: Any = None,
1960	    same_work_groups: Any = None,
1961	    checkpoint_dir: Optional[str] = None,
1962	) -> tuple[Any, bool, int, int]:
1963	    """Seam entry point. OFF (``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT`` unset/0) => calls ``_call_outline`` exactly
1964	    as before and returns its result untouched (byte-identical legacy path — this function does
1965	    NOT even import the agent-loop machinery on the OFF path beyond this module's top-level
1966	    imports, which are cheap/side-effect-free). ON => seeds via the SAME ``_call_outline`` call
1967	    (using the CODE model per §9.1.8 lock), then runs the ``OutlineAgent`` loop over the seeded
1968	    plans, and returns an updated result of the SAME shape the caller already expects."""
1969	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _call_outline  # noqa: PLC0415
1970	
1971	    if not outline_agent_enabled():
1972	        return await _call_outline(
1973	            research_question, evidence, gen_model, outline_temperature, outline_max_tokens,
1974	            domain=domain, finding_clusters=finding_clusters,
1975	            deliverable_spec=deliverable_spec, scope_spec=scope_spec,
1976	            same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
1977	        )
1978	
1979	    seed_model = outliner_code_model()
1980	    parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok = await _call_outline(
1981	        research_question, evidence, seed_model, outline_temperature, outline_max_tokens,
1982	        domain=domain, finding_clusters=finding_clusters,
1983	        deliverable_spec=deliverable_spec, scope_spec=scope_spec,
1984	        same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
1985	    )
1986	    if not parse_result.plans:
1987	        logger.info("[outline_agent] seed produced zero plans — skipping agent loop (fail-open)")
1988	        return parse_result, retry_attempted, in_tok, out_tok
1989	
1990	    ev_store = {
1991	        str(row.get("evidence_id")): row for row in evidence
1992	        if isinstance(row, dict) and row.get("evidence_id")
1993	    }
1994	    basket_menu = None
1995	    try:
1996	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.outline_digest import build_outline_digest  # noqa: PLC0415
1997	        basket_menu = build_outline_digest(
1998	            evidence, finding_clusters or [], same_work_groups=same_work_groups,
1999	            prioritize_tier1=True,
2000	        )
2001	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — basket digest is an aid, never a hard requirement
2002	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] basket digest build failed (fail-open): %s", exc)
2003	
2004	    # Snapshot the SEED plan's per-section ev_ids BEFORE the loop runs, so a caller (and the W1
2005	    # acceptance harness) can measure whether the outline actually mutated instead of trusting a
2006	    # bare final count — and so this is the ACTUAL seed the loop started from, not a second
2007	    # independent (nondeterministic) outline call.
2008	    seed_ev_by_title = {p.title: list(p.ev_ids) for p in parse_result.plans}
2009	
2010	    # Iter-2 P2-1 fix (was mis-reported as "new_evidence_count=0" every run): ``OutlineWorkspace``
2011	    # is handed ``ev_store`` BY REFERENCE (not a copy) and the loop mutates it in place via
2012	    # ``search_more_evidence``'s fold-in, so ``final_ws.ev_store is ev_store`` — the SAME dict
2013	    # object. Any comparison of ``len(ev_store)`` computed AFTER the loop against
2014	    # ``len(final_ws.ev_store)`` is comparing an object against itself and is always 0. This also
2015	    # broke the "feed newly-fetched rows back into `evidence`" loop below (the exact same
2016	    # aliasing made every row look like it was "already in ev_store", so it silently fed back
2017	    # ZERO rows — the agent-fetched corpus never reached the caller's `evidence` list at all).
2018	    # Snapshot the id SET before the loop runs; every later comparison is against this snapshot,
2019	    # never against the (now-mutated) live dict.
2020	    ev_ids_before = set(ev_store.keys())
2021	    ev_store_size_before = len(ev_ids_before)
2022	
2023	    # Iter-2 fix: thread the deliverable's required-structure lock (if any) through to the
2024	    # workspace so `_tool_update_outline` (via `apply_revision_ops(required_titles=...)`) and the
2025	    # auto-assign fallback both respect it — an exact-N-in-order required structure must never
2026	    # silently grow a 5th section behind the caller's back. Mirrors `_call_outline`'s own
2027	    # `_spec_read(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])` read (dict OR object shape).
2028	    _required_titles = [
2029	        str(t).strip()
2030	        for t in (
2031	            deliverable_spec.get("required_sections", [])
2032	            if isinstance(deliverable_spec, dict)
2033	            else getattr(deliverable_spec, "required_sections", [])
2034	        ) or []
2035	        if str(t).strip()
2036	    ]
2037	    workspace = OutlineWorkspace(
2038	        research_question=research_question,
2039	        ev_store=ev_store,
2040	        outline_draft=list(parse_result.plans),
2041	        basket_menu=basket_menu,
2042	        checkpoint_dir=checkpoint_dir,
2043	        total_input_tokens=in_tok,
2044	        total_output_tokens=out_tok,
2045	        required_titles=_required_titles,
2046	    )
2047	    agent = OutlineAgent(workspace, domain=domain or None)
2048	    # W2 P0 fix (Fable-authoritative, 2026-07-11): this call used to be BARE. On the dense
2049	    # full-corpus real run a single glm-5.2 ``ReasoningFirstTruncationError`` (reasoning
2050	    # prelude alone exceeding the completion budget) — or ANY other exception, or the loop
2051	    # simply running past its own wall-clock without ``_decide``/``_run_checklist`` ever
2052	    # returning control — propagated straight out of ``agent.run()``, out of THIS function,
2053	    # and aborted the entire outline stage; the caller (``multi_section_generator``) then fell
2054	    # back to the PLAIN non-agentic outliner. That is a silent capability downgrade the operator
2055	    # never asked for (LAW II). The fix DEGRADES TO SEED instead: ``workspace`` is the exact
2056	    # same object ``agent`` was constructed with, and every mutation the loop makes (fold-ins,
2057	    # revisions, ledger updates) is applied to it IN PLACE and checkpointed after every turn —
2058	    # so on any failure ``workspace`` already holds the SEED outline merged with whatever
2059	    # agentic turns completed before the failure, never a blank/plain fallback. The outer
2060	    # ``asyncio.wait_for`` is belt-and-suspenders on top of the loop's own internal
2061	    # turns/wall-clock check (a single hung LLM call inside one turn must not be able to wedge
2062	    # the whole stage past the caller's own upstream timeout).
2063	    degraded_to_seed = False
2064	    degrade_reason = ""
2065	    try:
2066	        final_ws = await asyncio.wait_for(
2067	            agent.run(),
2068	            timeout=agent.wall_seconds + _env_int(
2069	                "PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS",
2070	                PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_RUN_TIMEOUT_GRACE_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
2071	            ),
2072	        )
2073	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — degrade to SEED, never abort the outline stage
2074	        degraded_to_seed = True
2075	        degrade_reason = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
2076	        workspace.disclose(
2077	            f"agentic outline loop ABORTED after {workspace.turn} completed turn(s) "
2078	            f"({degrade_reason}) — DEGRADING TO SEED outline merged with whatever agentic "
2079	            "turns completed before the failure (never falls back to plain non-agentic "
2080	            "outline)"
2081	        )
2082	        workspace.checkpoint(event="agent_run_aborted_degrade_to_seed")
2083	        logger.warning(
2084	            "[outline_agent] agent.run() aborted, degrading to seed+partial-turns state: %s",
2085	            degrade_reason,
2086	        )
2087	        final_ws = workspace
2088	
2089	    parse_result.plans = list(final_ws.outline_draft)  # type: ignore[assignment]
2090	    parse_result.digest_stats = dict(parse_result.digest_stats or {})
2091	    parse_result.digest_stats["outline_agent"] = {
2092	        "cp4_used": "agentic-degraded-seed" if degraded_to_seed else "agentic",
2093	        "degraded_to_seed": degraded_to_seed,
2094	        "degrade_reason": degrade_reason,
2095	        "turns": final_ws.turn,
2096	        "elapsed_seconds": round(final_ws.elapsed_seconds(), 1),
2097	        "ev_store_size": len(final_ws.ev_store),
2098	        "ev_store_size_at_seed": ev_store_size_before,
2099	        "new_evidence_count": len(final_ws.ev_store) - ev_store_size_before,
2100	        "gap_ledger": final_ws.gap_ledger.as_list(),
2101	        "unfilled_gaps": [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in final_ws.gap_ledger.unfilled],
2102	        "disclosures": list(final_ws.disclosures),
2103	        # W4: the computed values a successful execute_python produced. Before this the
2104	        # entry-point return carried NO notebook payload at all, so the value died here.
2105	        # EXPLORATORY: every row is renderable=False; the report may render a computed
2106	        # number ONLY through the verified [#calc:] lane. Manifest/telemetry only — grep
2107	        # confirms digest_stats never reaches a writer prompt.
2108	        "notebook_compute": list(final_ws.compute_results),
2109	        "seed_ev_by_title": seed_ev_by_title,
2110	        "final_ev_by_title": {
2111	            _plan_field(p, "title", ""): list(_plan_field(p, "ev_ids", []) or [])
2112	            for p in final_ws.outline_draft
2113	        },
2114	    }
2115	    # Feed the enlarged evidence pool back so downstream fold-in (evidence_for_gen etc.) sees
2116	    # the new rows too — the caller reads `evidence` by reference in most call sites, but to
2117	    # stay honest under any calling convention this is ALSO returned via digest_stats above.
2118	    # (iter-2 fix: compare against the PRE-loop id snapshot, not the aliased live dict — see note
2119	    # above; this is the actual corpus feed-back path and was silently a no-op before this fix.)
2120	    for eid, row in final_ws.ev_store.items():
2121	        if eid and eid not in ev_ids_before:
2122	            evidence.append(row)
2123	
2124	    return parse_result, retry_attempted, final_ws.total_input_tokens, final_ws.total_output_tokens
