1	"""W4 regression tests for the REAL ``_wrap_execute_python`` body (tool_registry.py:547-640).
2	
3	WHY THIS FILE EXISTS (Fable, round-1 gate): every pre-existing execute_python test
4	(``tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py``:150,171) registers an inline async
5	fake UNDER THE REGISTRY NAME ``execute_python``, overwriting the real executor. Those tests
6	therefore prove only that the OutlineAgent DISPATCH hands a non-None code-model client to
7	whatever is registered — the executor body itself (the no-client guard at :561, the timeout
8	path, the success mapping of summary/statistics/insights into ToolResult) was executed by
9	ZERO tests. ``grep -rn generate_and_execute_analysis tests/`` returned nothing. The tool could
10	be bricked one layer below the dispatch without a single test going red.
11	
12	These tests call the REAL ``_wrap_execute_python`` and monkeypatch ONLY its collaborator,
13	``code_executor.generate_and_execute_analysis`` (the wrapper imports it lazily INSIDE the
14	function, so patching the attribute on the code_executor module is what the wrapper resolves).
15	No registry name is overwritten. No network, no sandbox.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import asyncio
21	
22	import pytest
23	
24	import src.polaris_graph.tools.code_executor as code_executor
25	from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolResult, _wrap_execute_python
26	
27	
28	class _Client:
29	    """Any truthy non-None object — the wrapper only checks ``if not client``."""
30	
31	
32	EV_STORE = {
33	    "ev_1": {"statement": "TELUS reported a 6.0% discount rate.", "source_url": "http://a"},
34	    "ev_2": {"statement": "Capex was $1.548 billion.", "source_url": "http://b"},
35	}
36	
37	
38	def _patch_codegen(monkeypatch, fn) -> list[dict]:
39	    """Patch the REAL collaborator and record the kwargs the real wrapper passes it."""
40	    calls: list[dict] = []
41	
42	    async def _spy(**kwargs):
43	        calls.append(kwargs)
44	        return await fn(**kwargs)
45	
46	    monkeypatch.setattr(code_executor, "generate_and_execute_analysis", _spy)
47	    return calls
48	
49	
50	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (a) success
51	
52	
53	def test_success_maps_summary_statistics_and_insights_into_toolresult(monkeypatch):
54	    """The success mapping at tool_registry.py:~620-640 is what makes the tool net-POSITIVE.
55	
56	    If it drops ``statistics``, a computed number is silently lost and the planner sees an
57	    OK step with no number in it (exactly the W4 bug, one layer up).
58	    """
59	    async def _ok(**kwargs):
60	        return {
61	            "success": True,
62	            "result": {
63	                "summary": "The 30-year NPV is $1234567.89 given the cited discount rate.",
64	                "statistics": {"npv": 1234567.89, "discount_rate": 0.06},
65	                "insights": ["NPV is positive under every sourced discount rate."],
66	            },
67	            "charts": [{"image_base64": "iVBORw0KGgo="}],
68	        }
69	
70	    calls = _patch_codegen(monkeypatch, _ok)
71	
72	    result = asyncio.run(_wrap_execute_python(
73	        evidence_store=EV_STORE,
74	        data_points=[{"evidence_id": "ev_1", "value": 6.0}, {"evidence_id": "ev_2", "value": 1.548}],
75	        client=_Client(),
76	        question="compute the 30-year NPV",
77	        research_context="TELUS 30yr NPV?",
78	    ))
79	
80	    assert isinstance(result, ToolResult)
81	    assert result.success is True
82	    assert result.tool_name == "execute_python"
83	    assert result.error is None
84	
85	    # THE load-bearing assertion: the computed number survives into ToolResult.statistics
86	    # VERBATIM (this is the field the notebook digest reads and the planner sees).
87	    assert result.statistics == {"npv": 1234567.89, "discount_rate": 0.06}
88	    assert result.insights == ["NPV is positive under every sourced discount rate."]
89	    assert "1234567.89" in result.markdown  # summary -> markdown, unmangled
90	    assert result.charts == [{"image_base64": "iVBORw0KGgo="}]
91	
92	    # _cite_inline stamped the evidence ids onto the number-bearing paragraph, and the ids
93	    # came from the data_points (which take precedence over the evidence_store branch).
94	    assert result.source_evidence_ids == ["ev_1", "ev_2"]
95	    assert "[CITE:ev_1]" in result.markdown
96	
97	    # the real wrapper actually invoked the real collaborator, with the caller's question
98	    assert len(calls) == 1
99	    assert calls[0]["analysis_question"] == "compute the 30-year NPV"
100	    assert calls[0]["research_context"] == "TELUS 30yr NPV?"
101	
102	
103	def test_success_falls_back_to_evidence_store_when_there_are_no_data_points(monkeypatch):
104	    """No data_points -> the wrapper builds input rows from the evidence_store (:573-581)."""
105	    async def _ok(**kwargs):
106	        return {"success": True, "result": {"summary": "n=2 rows seen.", "statistics": {"n": 2}}}
107	
108	    calls = _patch_codegen(monkeypatch, _ok)
109	
110	    result = asyncio.run(_wrap_execute_python(
111	        evidence_store=EV_STORE, data_points=[], client=_Client(),
112	    ))
113	
114	    assert result.success is True
115	    assert result.statistics == {"n": 2}
116	    assert result.source_evidence_ids == ["ev_1", "ev_2"]
117	    assert [r["evidence_id"] for r in calls[0]["evidence_data"]] == ["ev_1", "ev_2"]
118	
119	
120	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- (b) no client
121	
122	
123	def test_no_client_returns_the_no_llm_client_available_failure(monkeypatch):
124	    """tool_registry.py:561 — the guard the W3 dispatch fix existed to stop tripping.
